title: cerastium arvense var. boissieri
intro: Cerastium arvense var. boissieri, also known as field mouse-ear or Boissier's mouse-ear, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae). This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties, making it useful in treating various health issues such as wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of Cerastium arvense var. boissieri include its ability to soothe skin irritations, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing, as well as its potential to alleviate symptoms of colds and coughs. Herbal preparations of Cerastium arvense var. boissieri can be made into teas, infusions, or topical creams, and are often used in traditional folk medicine.
